blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Ughhh!
Shirone looked down at the writhing Lucia.
He wore a sorrowful expression.
The emotional disease that started in Tormia had reached the Vatican, carried by the wind.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It means it has almost circled the planet.
No one could escape it.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The guidelines from the World Health Organization can control the emotional disease to some extent. But for Lucia…
There was nothing left to give up.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, she can't give up. All the guidelines she follows in the name of God.
Glen was out of his mind.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: Lucia, just hold on a little longer! I'll cure you! I'll definitely take you to the Pyramid of Truth…!

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Oww! Aaaah!
Shirone asked coldly:

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What happened?
Earth Dragon Argan answered in a low voice:

blocks.speaker: Earth Dragon Argan
blocks.content: There are rumors that a pilgrim who approached the Pyramid of Truth was cured of the emotional disease.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I hadn't heard that.
If it were true, Seriel would have intervened.

blocks.speaker: Earth Dragon Argan
blocks.content: It seems the Kingdom of Paras controlled the information. But recently, more pilgrims have been coming. It's only a matter of time before the information spreads beyond the borders.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: …So the king allowed access.
It was a decision to reveal the truth of the pyramid before the situation became uncontrollable.
Ares spoke:

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Sorry for not telling you earlier. Initially, I wanted to explore the pyramid out of pure curiosity. Then the emotional disease broke out, and Glen and Lucia joined. Lucia's situation is special. She can't hold on any longer.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: There are a few treatments for the emotional disease. First, the World Health Organization's guidelines. And I've heard that someone named Giyorgi can alleviate the symptoms.
Shirone's eyes sharpened.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: But he's a demon. The Lami faith would never accept that method. Then I heard rumors about Yahweh. I don't know if it's true, but you also…
Ares hesitated for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: …have the ability to suppress the emotional disease?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes.
Glen turned his head and shouted:

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: Ares! Don't say such nonsense! A priest of the Lami faith receiving help from Yahweh!

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Aaaah!
Lucia couldn't hold back her screams.
Glen's face turned pale.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: Lucia! Lucia!
Watching this, Shirone said:

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But I can't cure it completely either. If she doesn't receive regular blessings from me, it will relapse.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: …I see.
Ares reached into his inner pocket.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Our last resort, the one we found in the southern continent, is this.
He pulled out a vial and a syringe.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Angel's Tears, Angel.
He presented all four methods to counter the emotional disease and said:

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: I brought it just in case. We can endure it, but Lucia won't give up on anything.
Even in pain, Lucia shook her head weakly at Ares's words.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: No, Glen. A priest shouldn't rely on such things… I'll endure it… Aaaah!
Everyone afflicted with the emotional disease knew it was not a pain they could endure.
Glen choked up:

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: Lucia, just this once. Take this medicine. Then I'll take you to the Pyramid of Truth…
Lucia gave a faint smile.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Glen, I will definitely return to the Vatican. The Pope… will acknowledge me.
The Papal States.
North of the Mediterranean lies the Vatican, the headquarters of the world's largest religion, Ramianism.
Shirone, Iruki, and Neide arrived in the Papal States early in the morning, having paused their pilgrimage across the central continent.
Neide glanced around nervously.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Do you think it'll be alright? If they realize you're Yahweh, the Vatican won't leave you alone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There's no choice. The Holy War is about to begin. If I don't meet the Pope now, I won't get another chance.
As Yahweh, who had fought demons during their pilgrimage, Shirone was now a globally recognized figure.
The Vatican could not ignore him, and they had frequently deployed the Holy Knights to hinder him.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The Vatican...
The tall spires, exuding an aura of reverence, soon came into view.
Extra! Extra!
Bald priests carrying bundles of newspapers distributed them to passersby.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What's going on?
The Vatican's official newspaper often reported significant events from dioceses worldwide, but a special edition was rare.
Iruki approached the priests on Shirone's behalf.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Can I have one, please?
The priest, focused solely on handing out newspapers, passed one to him without a glance.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Hmm.
Iruki scanned the headline: 'An Impending Holy War: World Leaders Stake Humanity's Future on God's Decree.'

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: So, it's come to this.
The Holy War would determine humanity's leading nations.
Public opinion largely believed this would be the last Holy War, given the dire global circumstances.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Twelve nations will participate this time.
Eligibility was based on factors like GDP, population, and military strength.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: In the past, the Triumvirate Nations led the Holy War as permanent council members. This time, the 12 nations will compete as non-permanent members, with Pope Constantine observing. Once a leading nation is chosen, it will be officially recognized by Ramianism.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: The leading nation will be decided by power dynamics, but religion ensures rapid acceptance of the results worldwide. With the Pope's declarations protected under international law, it's a strategic move.
Ramianism's influence was so pervasive that even non-believers equated 'God' with the Ramian God.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: It's a win for the Vatican too. In this apocalyptic era, where new religions are gaining traction, this is their chance to reclaim dominance over the religious sphere.
Shirone nodded.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I agree that uniting the world's powers is necessary. But it won't be as simple as it sounds...
Neide flipped through the newspaper.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Every article is about the Emotion Disease. Their intelligence network is incredible. Just reading this paper gives you a snapshot of global issues.
Then Shirone's eyes caught an article.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wait a second. Look at this...
The article's headline read: 'Where Is Priestess Lucia?'
Shirone read every word carefully.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Interesting. The Vatican's top priestess, once called the 'Essence of God,' has been excommunicated?
Lucia, a devout believer who rose to priesthood, was renowned as the Vatican's greatest priestess.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: She must've been extraordinarily gifted. Even infamous criminals reportedly knelt before her.
Shirone kept reading:

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She's cursed by demons. Not only did she injure High Priest Maximus, the Vatican's third-ranking official, but...
The article concluded with claims that the brilliant Priest Glen had succumbed to Lucia's temptations.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: A joint escape, huh? Since it's written from the Vatican's perspective, we can't trust it entirely. It could've been a romantic elopement. High-ranking priests and paladins are bound by vows of celibacy, after all.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: But they're not high-ranking yet. Though they probably would've been someday. Shirone, why does this article matter?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I know them.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: A simultaneous event?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. They're in the Paras Kingdom right now. I was wondering why they were so fixated on the Pyramid of Truth...
The Vatican.
Atop the highest spire, Pope Constantine overlooked the Papal States.
Knock on the door.
A communication priest from the Diocesan Administration arrived.

blocks.speaker: Communication Priest
blocks.content: Your Holiness, the special edition has been distributed. Soon, the entire world will know.

blocks.speaker: Pope Constantine
blocks.content: Well done.
He seemed to radiate light.
He was known as the world's strongest clergyman.
A peculiar title for a clergyman, but fitting in these demon-plagued times.

blocks.speaker: Communication Priest
blocks.content: He's the one who can stand against all demons and lead us to God's path.

blocks.speaker: Pope Constantine
blocks.content: I've read the special edition. It was well done.

blocks.speaker: Communication Priest
blocks.content: You honor us, Your Holiness. Your decision to follow WHO guidelines was a profound and inspiring choice.

blocks.speaker: Pope Constantine
blocks.content: And Lucia?
The priest flinched.

blocks.speaker: Communication Priest
blocks.content: It diverged slightly from my intentions. Not that I underestimate her abilities...

blocks.speaker: Communication Priest
blocks.content: My apologies. But if Your Holiness shows favoritism toward Lucia, it will undermine the Vatican's authority. After all, she—

blocks.speaker: Pope Constantine
blocks.content: Favoritism?
Realizing his mistake, the priest bowed deeply.

blocks.speaker: Communication Priest
blocks.content: I'm sorry!

blocks.speaker: Pope Constantine
blocks.content: It's alright. Everyone makes mistakes. Following God doesn't make us God. Many forget that, even High Priest Maximus.
The priest hesitated.
He knew it was risky for Constantine to criticize the Vatican's third-ranking official.

blocks.speaker: Pope Constantine
blocks.content: Haha! Does it matter? I'm not reprimanding anyone. Maximus, Lucia, even myself—we all make mistakes. Life is complicated.

blocks.speaker: Communication Priest
blocks.content: That's why we have doctrine, Your Holiness. To adhere to God's will. Lucia violated that doctrine, and her excommunication was justified.

blocks.speaker: Pope Constantine
blocks.content: Poor girl...
Constantine's eyes gleamed as he asked:

blocks.speaker: Pope Constantine
blocks.content: Tell me, why do you believe in God?

blocks.speaker: Communication Priest
blocks.content: ...Pardon me?
Such a question seemed almost blasphemous coming from the Pope himself.

blocks.speaker: Pope Constantine
blocks.content: If we wish to create a good world, isn't it enough to simply live kindly? Even if God created us, isn't it enough to live gratefully? Why, then, do we believe in God?
Finally, the priest's expression turned serious.

blocks.speaker: Communication Priest
blocks.content: I don't know...

blocks.speaker: Pope Constantine
blocks.content: Because we hope this isn't the end.

blocks.speaker: Pope Constantine
blocks.content: We believe in God because we yearn for eternity, seeking to transcend fleeting emptiness. That is the task given to us as religious leaders.
The priest shrank slightly under the weight of his words.

blocks.speaker: Pope Constantine
blocks.content: People easily forget death, but we cannot. Clergy aren't responsible for life—we're responsible for death.
Pope Constantine spoke about the certainty of endlessness.

blocks.speaker: Pope Constantine
blocks.content: Can you take responsibility for another's death? Can I take responsibility for humanity's death?

blocks.speaker: Pope Constantine
blocks.content: The one who accepts responsibility will be the one used by God.

blocks.speaker: Pope Constantine
blocks.content: I wish to see Lucia. And Glen, too.
After hesitating, the priest finally bowed and said:

blocks.speaker: Communication Priest
blocks.content: I'll prepare an article for the next issue.
Nagnag Oasis.
Lucia's screams were so harrowing that everyone in the tavern had fled, leaving only Shirone, Ares, and their companions anxiously waiting for her decision.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: No. The pain won't stop. Lucia won't abandon anything.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Angel.
Before Ares could act, Glen grabbed the syringe and injected the substance himself.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Glen, I...

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: I'm sorry, Lucia.
Unable to watch any longer, Glen pressed the needle into her frail arm.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: No! No!
Even as she writhed in pain, her resistance was fierce.
Then, suddenly, her eyes widened, and she gasped deeply.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Ahhhhhh!
Brilliant light enveloped her.
The veins protruding on her face began to subside.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Shirone...
Ares stepped back.
Glen turned toward Shirone, his face filled with astonishment.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: Yahweh...
A Miracle Stream was flowing.
Γùå Γùå Γùå
Glen frowned.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: What are you doing?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Lucia will be fine for now.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: I mean, what are you doing! Do you think she endured this because of the pain?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's better than Angel.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: You're wrong! I'd rather use drugs than accept help from a heretic. In the Lami faith, we must never…

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Glen.
Lucia's changed voice made Glen flinch.
He turned his head.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: I'm okay now.
Seeing the calm in her eyes, Glen couldn't say anything.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: Why?
He clenched his fist.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I know that Yahweh's criteria for treating the emotional disease are those who fight for the world.
Without such criteria, people all over the world would cling to Yahweh.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: But we are priests of the Lami faith. Helping you means denying our faith.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You don't need to deny it. I just suppressed Lucia's emotional disease.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: You broke your own rules! If you save Lucia, you have to save others too! But you can't do that. The whole world will condemn you!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then let them condemn me.
Glen's expression turned blank.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The important thing is to achieve better results, not to stick to my rules. I'm not doing this to be loved or recognized by anyone. I just…
He had one wish.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I just want everyone to be happy.
A moment of silence followed.
The apostles watched Shirone with reverence.
Glen bowed his head.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: Damn…
A hand rested on his shoulder.
When he turned his head, he saw Lucia, supported by Ares, smiling.
Her expression said it all.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: I'm sorry.
Glen turned back to Shirone and said:

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: I lost my reason in my haste. Give me a chance to explain. Lucia and I…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I know. I read about you in the Vatican's official newspaper. I heard you were excommunicated.
Lucia spoke:

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Glen and I are in love. But to become senior priests, we must follow the vow of celibacy. High Priest Maximus told me to break up with Glen. I got angry, and then…
She raised her left hand.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: A new ability manifested.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ability?
Glen explained:

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: Lucia has incredibly powerful abilities, unmatched even in the Vatican. That's why the High Priest disliked me. Because Lucia's future was at risk because of me.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Glen.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: I'm not resentful. I would have done the same. Anyway, Lucia has an amazing ability. They call her the Right Hand of God.
Lucia approached Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: I'll show you.
Frigi and Argan started to move.
Shirone stopped them.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's okay. I want to know too.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: My right hand…
When Lucia gently grasped Shirone's wrist, all the strength drained from his body.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh?
Lucia supported Shirone and laid him on the ground.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: It relaxes all living beings to the extreme.
It wasn't confinement.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It feels like a pleasant exhaustion. I can't move a finger, but there's no anxiety.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: No one has ever escaped this state. Not even Yaksha or Banya.
It was indeed powerful.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But you can escape it.
As Shirone, deep in thought, raised his hand, Glen's eyes widened.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: How?
Lucia helped Shirone up and said:

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Scholars call it an extraordinary phenomenon, but in the Lami faith, we call it a miracle.
Both were matters of faith.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: I can touch the mind of the subject. It feels like water. That's where it is decided. Unlike the mind, the heart has no defenses, so to overcome the Right Hand of God, the heart itself must be strong.
Lucia looked bitter.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: When I grabbed your wrist, honestly, I was dizzy. It felt like I was sinking into the ocean, not just touching water.
She felt Yahweh's heart.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: I have reformed many villains with this ability. But during my scolding by High Priest Maximus, a new ability awakened, perhaps because of a bad heart.
Lucia raised her other hand.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: The Left Hand of the Devil.
The reason a priest of the Lami faith called it the Devil's Hand was because it was true.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Any living being touched by my left hand twists and crumples itself. I quickly withdrew my hand, but High Priest Maximus's elbow was broken.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: …That's incredible.
Lucia nodded at Shirone's honest response.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Yes. The strength of the ability matches the Right Hand of God. I won't deny the name Devil. I feel ashamed and nauseous, but…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's the nature of the ability.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Yes. Like the extraordinary phenomenon, the miracle is also a function of the heart. I was excommunicated for it, but I'm sure. If I can subdue the Devil's Left Hand, it will prove my faith.
That was her story.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Yahweh, no, Shirone. Our beliefs may differ, but I saw that your heart is not different from mine. So please…
She bowed her head.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Take me to the Pyramid of Truth.
Ares's beard lifted with his smile.
Glen looked away, muttering:

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: …It will be helpful.
Shirone exchanged glances with Kanis, Arin, and Zulu, then nodded.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Alright.
The Kingdom of Jive.
Two days before the Holy War, rulers from across the world began arriving in the Kingdom of Jive.
A towering man in his mid-thirties, seated on the throne with a finely groomed beard, declared:

blocks.speaker: King Gith
blocks.content: Tomorrow is the eve of the festival.
This was King Gith, the Steel King, who had overthrown King Mayer and ascended to the throne through an election.

blocks.speaker: King Gith
blocks.content: It will also be the day I become king of the world.
Jive's annual GDP stood at 3,224 trillion gold, with 44 trillion allocated to defense.
The nation boasted nine certified First-Class Mages and had produced the era's enlightened leader, Nane.

blocks.speaker: Satiel
blocks.content: Hey, King!
The doors burst open.
A towering archangel strode in.
Satiel, Archangel of Disintegration, dwarfed the humans around her.

blocks.speaker: Satiel
blocks.content: How much longer do you intend to make me wait? I'm eager to disintegrate Ikael.
A ripple of unease crossed Gith's otherwise composed expression.

blocks.speaker: King Gith
blocks.content: By tomorrow, you'll be free to act. Consider this a test of patience for your vengeance.

blocks.speaker: Satiel
blocks.content: Patience? I wasn't supposed to negotiate with humans in the first place. The angels under me could handle this easily.

blocks.speaker: King Gith
blocks.content: You may destroy the target, but you cannot annihilate what lies within. Trust me, Ikael will shed tears of blood before this is over.
The one thing Ikael could not endure was seeing Shirone fall into ruin—a task only humans could achieve.
This was why Satiel had allied with Jive.

blocks.speaker: King Gith
blocks.content: Everything is proceeding as planned.
The Holy War being hosted in Jive was no coincidence; the Triumvirate Nations had swiftly approved it.

blocks.speaker: King Gith
blocks.content: They likely have their intelligence networks in place, but knowing changes nothing. Angels don't break contracts.
Satiel's forces were already considered part of Jive's arsenal.

blocks.speaker: Official
blocks.content: Your Majesty, the participating nations are arriving. Tormia, Kashan, and Corona have crossed the border.

blocks.speaker: King Gith
blocks.content: Hmm.

blocks.speaker: King Gith
blocks.content: Feeling like the center of the world wasn't unpleasant.
Gith rose from his throne.

blocks.speaker: King Gith
blocks.content: Excellent. Let's go greet them, shall we?
The Procession of Nations.

blocks.speaker: Announcer
blocks.content: Presenting! The Kingdom of Tormia!
The gates of Jive's outer walls opened.
Tormia's delegation entered on horseback.
King Pony rode in the center, flanked by Shirone and the Guardian Corps, who formed a defensive barrier.
Captain Ozentr Rai issued orders:

blocks.speaker: Captain Ozentr Rai
blocks.content: Watch for snipers. React immediately to any suspicious activity. I'll take full responsibility for the outcome.

blocks.speaker: Subordinates
blocks.content: Yes, sir!
His subordinates scanned the area sharply.
An elderly voice chuckled nearby.

blocks.speaker: Merkodain Albino
blocks.content: The new captain seems tense.
This was Merkodain Albino, head of Tormia's think tank, Dragon Lightning.

blocks.speaker: Merkodain Albino
blocks.content: Don't expend all your energy at the start. Every nation here is effectively at war. Nobody pushes others off the tightrope.
Rai remained steadfast.

blocks.speaker: Captain Ozentr Rai
blocks.content: Tormia is a relatively small nation. Anything could happen. Don't worry—I won't lose focus.

blocks.speaker: Merkodain Albino
blocks.content: Reassuring.
Tormia's annual GDP was 1,700 trillion gold, with 24 trillion dedicated to defense.
The nation had three certified First-Class Mages, along with the era's benevolent figure Yahweh, the righteous Miro, and exclusive technology for elemental bombs.

blocks.speaker: Merkodain Albino
blocks.content: A leadership nation, hmm?
Albino's smile twisted into something grotesque as he gazed beyond the Guardian Corps.

blocks.speaker: Merkodain Albino
blocks.content: A gamble worth taking.
Meanwhile, Shirone's attention was drawn to the procession from the Kingdom of Corona passing in the distance.
Corona's entourage included numerous residents of the Ivory Tower, along with some of the Stars.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Minerva isn't here. Is she too preoccupied holding back Baal? Or is she…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: …keeping an eye on Sing?

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: Shirone.
Lupist approached, accompanied by Plu and Dante.

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: What about Gaold?
If Miro and Gaold joined Tormia, the balance of power would shift drastically.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There's no connection yet. Let's wait a bit longer. If he were dead, I wouldn't be here either.

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: ...True.
Shirone glanced behind him.
A procession of officials was followed by the military, and behind them trailed the entertainers.
Court musician Reina led the group, alongside Maya and Kaiden.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Where's Kuan? Is he with the teachers?
Among the educators attending the Humanity's Future Forum was Siena.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He wouldn't approach me in person. Then again, maybe he's closer than I realize.
In the Skies.
Contrary to Shirone's thoughts, Kuan had left the Tormia procession and was high above, observing the Kashan Empire's convoy.
His gaze locked onto the Wind General guarding Wuorin.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Time to cut their momentum.
Meanwhile, Captain Kido of the Guardian Corps frowned.

blocks.speaker: Captain Kido
blocks.content: This is irritating. Feels like someone's watching us from above. Should I bring them down?
The Wind General remained silent.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Let it go. You won't catch them anyway.

blocks.speaker: Captain Kido
blocks.content: Are you mocking me? Why even appoint me as captain if this is how you treat me?

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Haha! Because I'm bored. This Holy War looks stressful enough as it is.
As Kido glared, another officer, Gando, pointed ahead.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: Your Majesty, look there.
In the vast square leading to Jive's inner city, the delegations of Tormia, Corona, and Kashan converged.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Who enters first?
King Woodlai of Corona smiled diplomatically.

blocks.speaker: King Woodlai
blocks.content: Shall we avoid unnecessary pride contests and enter in the order we arrived?
Corona had arrived five seconds earlier.
Wuorin smirked.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Still, this is the Holy War. Shouldn't there be some special treatment? Kashan will go first.

blocks.speaker: King Woodlai
blocks.content: Special treatment?
Woodlai's lips curled.

blocks.speaker: King Woodlai
blocks.content: True, this is Corona's first Holy War. But surely the Empress of Kashan knows it's not because of weak national power.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Is that so? I wasn't aware there were nations stronger than Kashan.

blocks.speaker: King Woodlai
blocks.content: Haha! Fine, if the fallen empire's princess insists on preserving her pride, I'll indulge her this once.
Just then, smoke rose behind Wuorin.
Everyone's heart sank.

blocks.speaker: Crowd
blocks.content: What's going on?
Why hadn't anyone noticed?
Seated behind Wuorin was a man calmly inhaling a narcotic asker.
Phew.
Shirone's eyes sharpened.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Satan.
It was Gustav Havitz.
